meta.title: Gallery of the masters
meta.subtitle: The voices of the way
meta.intro: From the ninth century to our own day — the figures who carried, thought and transmitted Sufism. Each one can be approached through his life, his word, his works.

auteurs.tagline: Pure love, before fear and before hope.
auteurs.citation: “Lord, if I worship You out of fear of hell, burn me in hell. If I worship You in hope of paradise, exclude me from it. But if I worship You for Yourself, do not deprive me of Your eternal beauty.”

auteurs.tagline: Spiritual intoxication — the first of the shaṭḥ.
auteurs.citation: “Subḥānī! Mā aʿẓama shaʾnī!” — “Glory be to me! How great is my majesty!”

auteurs.tagline: The lord of the way — sayyid al-ṭāʾifa.
auteurs.citation: “Sufism is that God should make you die to yourself and make you live in Him.”

auteurs.tagline: Anā-l-Ḥaqq — the word that led him to the gallows.
auteurs.citation: “I am the Truth” — Anā-l-Ḥaqq.

auteurs.tagline: The Epistle — the great Sufi synthesis of the eleventh century.
auteurs.citation: “Sufism is a science whose foundations are actions, and whose fruits are states.”

auteurs.tagline: The revival of the religious sciences — a bridge between the Law and the Way.
auteurs.citation: “If you have not found God in prayer, where will you find Him?”

auteurs.tagline: Oriental wisdom — founder of the Illuminationist school (ishrāqī).
auteurs.citation: “Light, by itself, is manifest to itself.”

auteurs.tagline: The shaykh al-akbar — the greatest of the masters.
auteurs.citation: “My heart has become capable of every form.”

auteurs.tagline: The Ḥikam — Shādhilī wisdom condensed into aphorisms.
auteurs.citation: “How could your heart be illumined while the images of this world are engraved upon its mirror?”

auteurs.tagline: The master of Konya — the Mathnawī, an ocean of poetry.
auteurs.citation: “Come, whoever you are — pagan, idolater, fire-worshipper. Come, our door is not a door of despair.”

auteurs.tagline: The Conference of the Birds — the quest for the Simorgh.
auteurs.citation: “The birds then understood that the Simorgh was themselves — sī morgh, thirty birds.”

auteurs.tagline: The Gulistan — Sufi wisdom in a garden and a rose-bed.
auteurs.citation: “The children of Adam are limbs of one same body — created of one same essence.”

auteurs.tagline: The dervish of the people — the way in the popular Turkish tongue.
auteurs.citation: “Let us love and be loved — this world remains to no one.”

auteurs.tagline: The Dīwān — wine and the veiled divine beauty.
auteurs.citation: “From the day of alast until the Day of Judgement, my covenant with the Beloved has not changed by a single letter.”

auteurs.tagline: Al-Insān al-Kāmil — the doctrine of the Perfect Man, heir of Ibn ʿArabī.
auteurs.citation: “The Perfect Man is the mirror in which God sees Himself.”

auteurs.tagline: Transcendent wisdom — al-ḥikma al-mutaʿāliya, a synthesis of ishrāq and Ibn ʿArabī.
auteurs.citation: “Existence (al-wujūd) is what unfolds in degrees, from the densest to the most subtle.”

auteurs.tagline: The renewer of the second millennium — waḥdat al-shuhūd against waḥdat al-wujūd.
auteurs.citation: “Unity is not in being, but in the witnessing of the servant.”

auteurs.tagline: The resistance fighter become a saint of Ibn ʿArabī — Damascus, in exile.
auteurs.citation: “My God, You have given me the sword and the pen. Give me now the silence.”

auteurs.tagline: The founder of the Tijāniyya — its influence in West Africa.
auteurs.citation: “Our way is easy through love, and difficult through exclusivity.”

auteurs.tagline: The master through whom Sufism reached the West.
auteurs.citation: “The way is simpler than it is said to be. What is difficult is to make oneself simple.”

auteurs.tagline: The metaphysician who restored Tradition to the West.
auteurs.citation: “Pure metaphysics admits of no divisions.”

auteurs.tagline: The transcendent unity of religions — the most metaphysical wing of Tradition.
auteurs.citation: “All that exists is necessarily the reflection of something that is in God.”

auteurs.tagline: Sacred art, cosmology, Sufi alchemy explained to the West.
auteurs.citation: “Sacred art is the sensible expression of the sacred.”

auteurs.name_ar: مَارْتِن لِينْغْز · أَبُو بَكْر سِرَاج الدِّين
auteurs.name_tr: Martin Lings · Abū Bakr Sirāj al-Dīn
auteurs.tagline: The biographer of al-ʿAlawī and of Muḥammad — the one who knew how to transmit.
auteurs.citation: “His mere presence in a room changed everything.”

auteurs.tagline: The great conveyor of Ibn ʿArabī into French.
auteurs.citation: “The whole thought of Ibn ʿArabī rests upon an absolute fidelity to the Quran.”

auteurs.tagline: The philosopher of the imaginal — conveyor of Iranian Sufism.
auteurs.citation: “The imaginal is not the imaginary — it is the intermediate world where bodies become spiritual.”

auteurs.tagline: The living heir of the perennial tradition — philosophy of nature, scientia sacra.
auteurs.citation: “The forgetting of God is the forgetting of the self.”

auteurs.tagline: The one who introduced Ibn ʿArabī to the English-speaking world — a disciple of Nasr.
auteurs.citation: “The challenge is not to understand Ibn ʿArabī — it is to think as he did.”
